title: zerna pumpelliana subsp. arctica
intro: Zerna pumpelliana subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic Mountain Avens, is a plant species native to the Arctic regions of North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Zerna pumpelliana subsp. arctica include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
